sentences.sentence: One of the first great intellectual feats of a young child is learning how to talk, closely followed by learning how to count.
sentences.translation: 幼儿最早取得的重大智力成就之一，是学会说话，紧随其后的则是学会数数。

structure.text: One of the first great intellectual feats of a young child
closely followed by learning how to count

difficult_points: 主语中心是 One，of 后说明它属于幼儿最早的重大智力成就之一，因此系动词用 is。
learning how to talk 是动名词短语作表语；how to talk 是“疑问词 + 不定式”结构。
closely followed by... 是过去分词补充语，说明学会数数紧接在学说话之后。

expressions.text: one of the first great feats

sentences.sentence: From earliest childhood we are so bound up with our system of numeration that it is a feat of imagination to consider the problems faced by early humans who had not yet developed this facility.
sentences.translation: 我们从幼年起就与计数体系紧密相连，以至于要设想尚未发展出这种能力的早期人类所面对的问题，本身就需要非凡的想象力。

structure.text: are so bound up with our system of numeration
that it is a feat of imagination to consider the problems faced by early humans who had not yet developed this facility
who had not yet developed this facility

difficult_points: so... that... 构成结果结构，表示我们对计数体系太熟悉，以至于很难想象没有它的处境。
that 从句中的 it 是形式主语，真正主语是 to consider...。
faced by... 修饰 problems；who 修饰 early humans，this facility 指计数能力。

sentences.sentence: Careful consideration of our system of numeration leads to the conviction that, rather than being a facility that comes naturally to a person, it is one of the great and remarkable achievements of the human race.
sentences.translation: 仔细思考我们的计数体系便会使人确信：它并非一种与生俱来的能力，而是人类伟大而非凡的成就之一。

structure.text: Careful consideration of our system of numeration
that, rather than being a facility that comes naturally to a person, it is one of the great and remarkable achievements of the human race
rather than being a facility that comes naturally to a person
that comes naturally to a person
it is one of the great and remarkable achievements of the human race

difficult_points: that 从句同位说明 conviction 的具体内容，而不是修饰 numeration。
rather than being... 与主句的 is one of... 构成对比，it 指 our system of numeration。
comes naturally to 表示无需刻意学习便自然具备。

sentences.sentence: It is impossible to learn the sequence of events that led to our developing the concept of number.
sentences.translation: 我们已经不可能弄清促使人类形成数概念的一系列事件究竟如何发生。

structure.text: to learn the sequence of events
that led to our developing the concept of number

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，to learn... 是真正主语。
that 修饰 events；lead to 中 to 是介词，后接 our developing，our 是动名词的逻辑主语。

sentences.sentence: Even the earliest of tribes had a system of numeration that, if not advanced, was sufficient for the tasks that they had to perform.
sentences.translation: 即便是最早期的部落也拥有一套计数体系；这套体系即使并不先进，也足以应付他们必须完成的任务。

structure.text: that, if not advanced, was sufficient for the tasks that they had to perform

difficult_points: 第一个 that 修饰 a system of numeration，并在从句中作主语。
if not advanced 是省略结构，可补为 if it was not advanced，带有让步意味“即使不先进”。
第二个 that 修饰 tasks；they 指 the earliest tribes。

sentences.sentence: Our ancestors had little use for actual numbers; instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough? rather than How many? when they were engaged in food gathering, for example.
sentences.translation: 我们的祖先很少需要使用确切数字；例如在采集食物时，他们考虑的更可能是“这些够不够？”，而不是“有多少？”。

structure.text: instead their considerations would have been more of the kind Is this enough? rather than How many?
when they were engaged in food gathering

difficult_points: 分号后的 instead 引出与使用确切数字相反的思考方式。
would have been 是对远古情况的推测；Is this enough? 与 How many? 是嵌入句中的两种问题类型。
be engaged in 后接 food gathering，表示从事食物采集。

expressions.meaning_zh: 很少需要；觉得……用处不大

sentences.sentence: However, when early humans first began to reflect on the nature of things around them, they discovered that they needed an idea of number simply to keep their thoughts in order.
sentences.translation: 然而，当早期人类开始思考周围事物的性质时，他们发现，仅仅为了让思绪有条理，自己也需要数字这一概念。

structure.text: when early humans first began to reflect on the nature of things around them
that they needed an idea of number simply to keep their thoughts in order
simply to keep their thoughts in order

difficult_points: when 从句说明人类产生数概念需求的认知阶段。
that 从句作 discovered 的宾语；两个 they 都指 early humans。
to keep their thoughts in order 是目的状语，说明需要数概念首先是为了整理思维。

sentences.sentence: As they began to settle, grow plants and herd animals, the need for a sophisticated number system became paramount.
sentences.translation: 随着他们开始定居、种植作物和放牧牲畜，对复杂计数体系的需求变得至关重要。

structure.text: As they began to settle, grow plants and herd animals
settle, grow plants and herd animals
the need for a sophisticated number system

difficult_points: As 引导伴随发展的时间从句，表示生活方式变化与数字需求增强同步发生。
settle、grow 和 herd 是 begin to 后并列的三个动作，后两项省略 to。
paramount 表示重要性最高、首要，而不仅是一般的“重要”。

sentences.sentence: It will never be known how and when this numeration ability developed, but it is certain that numeration was well developed by the time humans had formed even semi-permanent settlements.
sentences.translation: 这种计数能力究竟如何、何时形成，人们永远无从知晓；但可以确定的是，甚至在人类形成半永久定居点时，计数能力就已经相当成熟。

structure.text: how and when this numeration ability developed
but it is certain that numeration was well developed by the time humans had formed even semi-permanent settlements
that numeration was well developed by the time humans had formed even semi-permanent settlements
by the time humans had formed even semi-permanent settlements

difficult_points: 两个 it 都是形式主语：前者对应 how and when 从句，后者对应 that 从句。
by the time 引导时间从句，表示到人类建立半永久定居点时，计数体系已经发展完善。
had formed 是过去完成时，用来标示相对于叙述参照点已完成的定居过程。

sentences.sentence: Evidence of early stages of arithmetic and numeration can be readily found.
sentences.translation: 算术和计数早期发展阶段的证据很容易找到。

structure.text: Evidence of early stages of arithmetic and numeration

difficult_points: of early stages... 修饰 Evidence，说明证据涉及什么。
can be found 是情态动词加被动语态，readily 表示“很容易地”。

sentences.sentence: The indigenous peoples of Tasmania were only able to count one, two, many; those of South Africa counted one, two, two and one, two twos, two twos and one, and so on.
sentences.translation: 塔斯马尼亚原住民只能数“一、二、许多”；南非原住民则数“一、二、二加一、两个二、两个二加一”，依此类推。

structure.text: were only able to count one, two, many
counted one, two, two and one, two twos, two twos and one, and so on

difficult_points: 分号连接两个地区原住民计数方式的对比。
those 代替 the indigenous peoples，避免重复；of South Africa 限定所指人群。
two twos 表示两个“二”，即四；and so on 表示按这种组合继续。

sentences.sentence: But in real situations the number and words are often accompanied by gestures to help resolve any confusion.
sentences.translation: 但在实际情境中，数字和词语往往还伴有手势，以帮助消除可能的混淆。
difficult_points: are accompanied by 是被动结构，说明口头数字和词语会配合手势使用。
to help resolve... 是目的状语；help 后的 resolve 省略了 to。

sentences.sentence: For example, when using the one, two, many type of system, the word many would mean, Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you.
sentences.translation: 例如，使用“一、二、许多”这种计数体系时，“许多”这个词的意思就是：“看我的手，数数我给你看了多少根手指。”

structure.text: when using the one, two, many type of system
Look at my hands and see how many fingers I am showing you
how many fingers I am showing you

difficult_points: when using... 是省略了使用者主语的时间状语结构，表示在采用这种体系时。
逗号后的 Look... 是 many 在实际交流中代表的完整指令内容。
how many fingers... 是间接疑问从句，作 see 的宾语，使用陈述语序。

sentences.sentence: This basic approach is limited in the range of numbers that it can express, but this range will generally suffice when dealing with the simpler aspects of human existence.
sentences.translation: 这种基本方法能表达的数字范围有限，但在人类生活较简单的方面，这一范围通常已经够用。

structure.text: but this range will generally suffice
when dealing with the simpler aspects of human existence

difficult_points: that 修饰 the range of numbers，并在从句中作 express 的宾语；it 指 this basic approach。
suffice 是不及物动词，表示“足够、够用”。
when dealing with... 是省略主语的状语结构，说明在哪类生活需求中这一范围够用。

sentences.sentence: The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers is not really surprising.
sentences.translation: 有些文化缺乏处理大数的能力，其实并不令人惊讶。

structure.text: The lack of ability of some cultures to deal with large numbers

difficult_points: 主语中心词是 The lack，of ability 和 of some cultures 逐层限定这种缺失。
to deal with large numbers 后置说明 ability 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: European languages, when traced back to their earlier version, are very poor in number words and expressions.
sentences.translation: 欧洲语言若追溯到早期形态，会发现其中表示数字的词语和表达极为贫乏。

structure.text: when traced back to their earlier version
very poor in number words and expressions

difficult_points: when traced back... 是省略 they are 的被动状语从句，逻辑主语为 European languages。
be poor in 表示在某方面缺乏，number 同时修饰 words and expressions。

sentences.sentence: The ancient Gothic word for ten, tachund, is used to express the number 100 as tachund tachund.
sentences.translation: 古哥特语中表示“十”的词 tachund，通过说 tachund tachund 来表示数字100。

structure.text: The ancient Gothic word for ten

difficult_points: tachund 是 The ancient Gothic word for ten 的同位语。
is used to express 是被动结构，说明该词被用来表示什么；as 后给出实际表达形式。

sentences.sentence: By the seventh century, the word teon had become interchangeable with the tachund or hund of the Anglo-Saxon language, and so 100 was denoted as hund teontig, or ten times ten.
sentences.translation: 到7世纪时，teon 已可与盎格鲁-撒克逊语中的 tachund 或 hund 互换使用，因此100被表示为 hund teontig，也就是“十乘十”。

structure.text: interchangeable with the tachund or hund of the Anglo-Saxon language
and so 100 was denoted as hund teontig, or ten times ten

difficult_points: had become 是过去完成时，表示到7世纪这一时间点时变化已经完成。
and so 引出结果；was denoted as 是被动语态，表示100采用何种说法。
ten times ten 是 hund teontig 的同位解释。

sentences.sentence: The average person in the seventh century in Europe was not as familiar with numbers as we are today.
sentences.translation: 7世纪的普通欧洲人不像今天的我们这样熟悉数字。

structure.text: The average person in the seventh century in Europe
as familiar with numbers as we are today

difficult_points: not as... as... 是否定同级比较，表示当时普通人对数字的熟悉程度低于现代人。
第二个 as 后是比较从句 we are today，其中 are 后省略 familiar with numbers。

sentences.sentence: In fact, to qualify as a witness in a court of law a man had to be able to count to nine!
sentences.translation: 事实上，当时一个人要想有资格在法庭上作证，必须能够数到九！

structure.text: to qualify as a witness in a court of law
had to be able to count to nine

difficult_points: to qualify... 位于句首作目的状语，表示为了获得证人资格。
had to be able to 把义务与能力结合起来，表示法律要求一个人具备数到九的能力。

sentences.sentence: Perhaps the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number is not the ability to count, but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects.
sentences.translation: 培养数感最根本的一步，或许不是拥有数数能力，而是认识到数字其实是一种抽象概念，并非依附于某一组具体物体的简单标签。

structure.text: the most fundamental step in developing a sense of number
but rather to see that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects
that a number is really an abstract idea instead of a simple attachment to a group of particular objects

difficult_points: not... but rather... 对比“会数数”和“理解数字的抽象性”，后者才被视为根本步骤。
in developing... 修饰 step；to count 修饰 ability。
that 从句作 see 的宾语；instead of 对比 abstract idea 与对具体物体的依附。

sentences.sentence: It must have been within the grasp of the earliest humans to conceive that four birds are distinct from two birds; however, it is not an elementary step to associate the number 4, as connected with four birds, to the number 4, as connected with four rocks.
sentences.translation: 早期人类想必能够理解四只鸟不同于两只鸟；然而，要把与四只鸟相关的数字4同与四块石头相关的数字4联系起来，却并不是一个简单的认知步骤。

structure.text: must have been within the grasp of the earliest humans
to conceive that four birds are distinct from two birds
that four birds are distinct from two birds
however, it is not an elementary step to associate the number 4, as connected with four birds, to the number 4, as connected with four rocks
to associate the number 4, as connected with four birds, to the number 4, as connected with four rocks

difficult_points: 前后两个 it 都是形式主语，真正主语分别是两个不定式短语。
must have been 表示对远古能力的有把握推测；within the grasp of 表示在理解能力范围内。
两个 as connected with... 都是省略结构，分别限定数字4与鸟、石头的关联情境。

sentences.sentence: Associating a number as one of the qualities of a specific object is a great hindrance to the development of a true number sense.
sentences.translation: 把数字看作某个特定物体的一项属性，会严重阻碍真正数感的发展。

structure.text: Associating a number as one of the qualities of a specific object
a great hindrance to the development of a true number sense

difficult_points: Associating... 是动名词短语作主语，表示一种认知方式。
associate A as B 表示把 A 视作 B；a hindrance to 中 to 是介词，后接名词 development。

sentences.sentence: When the number 4 can be registered in the mind as a specific word, independent of the object being referenced, the individual is ready to take the first step toward the development of a notational system for numbers and, from there, to arithmetic.
sentences.translation: 当数字4能够作为一个特定词语被记入脑中，不再依赖所指的具体物体时，个体就准备好迈出第一步，发展数字符号系统，并由此走向算术。

structure.text: When the number 4 can be registered in the mind as a specific word, independent of the object being referenced
independent of the object being referenced
the first step toward the development of a notational system for numbers and, from there, to arithmetic

difficult_points: When 从句兼有时间与条件含义，描述数字摆脱具体物体后认知达到的阶段。
being referenced 是动名词的被动式，the object 是其逻辑主语。
toward... and, from there, to arithmetic 表示先发展符号体系，再由此迈向算术。

sentences.sentence: Traces of the very first stages in the development of numeration can be seen in several living languages today.
sentences.translation: 如今，在几种仍在使用的语言中，可以看到计数体系最早发展阶段的痕迹。

structure.text: Traces of the very first stages in the development of numeration

difficult_points: 主语中心词是 Traces，后面的 of 和 in 短语逐层说明是什么痕迹。
living languages 指今天仍有人使用的语言，而不是“有生命的语言”。

sentences.sentence: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia contains seven distinct sets of words for numbers according to the class of the item being counted: for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated.
sentences.translation: 不列颠哥伦比亚省钦西安语的计数体系，会根据被计数物品的类别使用七套不同的数字词：分别用于数扁平物体和动物、圆形物体和时间、人、长形物体和树木、独木舟、度量，以及不针对特定物体的抽象计数。

structure.text: The numeration system of the Tsimshian language in British Columbia
seven distinct sets of words for numbers
according to the class of the item being counted
for counting flat objects and animals, for round objects and time, for people, for long objects and trees, for canoes, for measures, and for counting when no particular object is being numerated
when no particular object is being numerated

difficult_points: according to... 说明七套词汇是按被计数对象类别区分的；being counted 被动修饰 item。
冒号后七个 for 短语逐项列举各套数字词的适用对象。
最后的 when 从句指不对任何特定物体计数的抽象场景，is being numerated 是进行时被动。

sentences.sentence: It seems that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system.
sentences.translation: 最后一套似乎是后来才发展出来的，而前六套则保留着更古老体系的遗迹。

structure.text: that the last is a later development while the first six groups show the relics of an older system
while the first six groups show the relics of an older system

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，that 从句说明看起来成立的判断。
the last 指第七套不针对具体物体的计数词；the first six groups 指前六套。
while 表示对比，而不是单纯表示同时发生。

sentences.sentence: This diversity of number names can also be found in some widely used languages such as Japanese.
sentences.translation: 这种数字名称的多样性也可见于日语等一些广泛使用的语言。
difficult_points: This diversity 回指同一语言依据对象类别使用多套数字词的现象。
widely used 是过去分词短语作前置修饰；such as Japanese 给出具体例子。

sentences.sentence: Intermixed with the development of a number sense is the development of an ability to count.
sentences.translation: 与数感的发展交织在一起的，是数数能力的发展。

structure.text: Intermixed with the development of a number sense
the development of an ability to count

difficult_points: 这是表语前置造成的倒装，正常语序为 The development of an ability to count is intermixed with...。
Intermixed 是过去分词形容词，表示两种发展过程相互交织。

sentences.sentence: Counting is not directly related to the formation of a number concept because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers.
sentences.translation: 数数并不直接等同于数概念的形成，因为人们可以把要数的物品与一堆石子、玉米粒或计数者的手指逐一对应来完成计数。

structure.text: the formation of a number concept
because it is possible to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers
to count by matching the items being counted against a group of pebbles, grains of corn, or the counter's fingers

difficult_points: because 从句解释数数为何不必依赖抽象数概念；其中 it 是形式主语，to count... 是真正主语。
by matching... 表示计数的方法；match A against B 指将待数物品与辅助物逐一对应。
being counted 是被动分词短语，修饰 the items。

sentences.sentence: These aids would have been indispensable to very early people who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid.
sentences.translation: 对于极早期的人类来说，这些辅助物想必不可或缺；没有某种实物辅助，他们会觉得计数过程根本无法完成。

structure.text: who would have found the process impossible without some form of mechanical aid

difficult_points: would have been 是对史前情况的推测，而不是与事实相反的条件结果。
who 修饰 very early people；find + 宾语 + impossible 表示“认为这一过程无法完成”。
without... 说明若缺少实物辅助，早期人类便难以计数。

sentences.sentence: Such aids, while different, are still used even by the most educated in today's society due to their convenience.
sentences.translation: 这类辅助工具虽然形式不同，但由于方便，即便是当今社会受教育程度最高的人也仍在使用。

structure.text: even by the most educated in today's society

difficult_points: while different 是省略 they are 的让步从句，表示现代辅助物与早期辅助物形式不同。
the most educated 是“the + 形容词”结构，泛指受教育程度最高的人。
their 回指 Such aids；due to 引出继续使用它们的原因。

sentences.sentence: All counting ultimately involves reference to something other than the things being counted.
sentences.translation: 一切计数归根结底都需要借助被计数物之外的某种参照物。

structure.text: other than the things being counted

difficult_points: reference to something 表示借助某物作为参照，而不是文献引用。
other than 限定 something 必须不同于被数的物品；being counted 被动修饰 the things。

sentences.sentence: At first it may have been grains or pebbles but now it is a memorised sequence of words that happen to be the names of the numbers.
sentences.translation: 最初，这种参照物可能是谷粒或石子；如今，它则是一串记在脑中的词语，而这些词碰巧就是数字的名称。

structure.text: but now it is a memorised sequence of words
that happen to be the names of the numbers

difficult_points: 前后两个 it 都回指上一句计数时借助的 something，即参照物。
may have been 表示对早期参照物的推测；but now 与 At first 构成时间对比。
that 修饰 words；happen to be 表示这些记忆中的词恰好就是数字名称。
